description: Create a content slide with a full-width horizontal timeline showing the evolution of robotics from 1950 to 2035, including historical milestones and future projections.

header.kicker: Technology Vision 2025 | AI: A Declaration of Autonomy
header.headline: When LLMs get their Bodies

rows.bar_label: Isaac Asimov’s I, Robot is published
Stanford Research Institute begins developing Shakey, a robot that can navigate complex environments and find and group boxes
Waseda University creates WABOT-1, the first full-scale anthropomorphic robot that can walk and grip objects
iRobot launches Roomba, a floor vacuuming robot
Willow Garage is founded and begins work on ROS (Robot Operating System) as the Linux of robotics
The DARPA Robotics Challenge finals highlight many remaining barriers to general-purpose robots
Boston Dynamics releases a viral video of its humanoid robot, Atlas
Google Research unveils RT-1, a transformer-based model for robotics
Stanford University, the University of California Berkeley, and Meta introduce ALOHA, a low-cost, open-source robotics system
Figure and OpenAI partner to release Figure 01, a robot that reasons
A few hospitals in Japan and Denmark become the first to use generalist robots
A consumer electronics company introduces a generalist robot prototype for limited household tasks
Humanoid and quadruped robot production surpasses 100,000 per year
Like app stores of the past, a market emerges to download new skills for generalist robots
A logistics company deploys humanoid robots to address labor shortages, delivering packages to even the most remote areas
